nodes.name: domain-graph.json
nodes.summary: Domain-level architecture graph for the Archie project, defining 7 domains (Annotation Authoring, Annotation Versioning, Media Pipeline, Storage and Persistence, UI Shell and Navigation, Input and Gesture, Publishing and Export) with entities, business rules, and cross-domain interactions derived from the full knowledge graph.

nodes.name: fingerprints.json
nodes.summary: Content-hash fingerprint cache for all 143 analyzed files in the Archie project, storing per-file structural data (functions, classes, imports, exports) and content hashes to enable incremental re-analysis by detecting file changes since the last scan.

nodes.name: knowledge-graph.json
nodes.summary: Complete knowledge graph output from the understand-anything analysis of the Archie project, containing nodes (files, functions, classes, configs, documents), edges (imports, contains, calls, configures, documents), layer definitions, and an interactive tour for codebase navigation.

nodes.name: meta.json
nodes.summary: Analysis metadata record tracking the last analysis timestamp (2026-05-25T05:56:00Z), git commit hash, schema version, and total analyzed file count (143) for the Archie project's understand-anything run.

nodes.name: CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: Context-mode MCP routing rules for the Archie project, detailing blocked commands, redirected tools, tool selection hierarchy, and output constraints for Claude sessions.

nodes.name: CONTEXT.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive design document for Archie, defining core vocabulary (Library, Exhibit, Object, Note, Layer), locked requirements (OSD+Annotorious, W3C WADM, IIIF), architectural through-line of source-before-projection, and UX decisions for the static-publishable annotation platform.

nodes.name: HANDOFF.md
nodes.summary: Session handoff document tracking Archie implementation progress across phases, documenting completed work (spine data model, extraction, Studio/Viewer apps, publish pipeline), known bugs (draw not creating notes), and remaining tasks requiring browser or human verification.

nodes.name: IMPLEMENTATION-STRATEGY.md
nodes.summary: Meta-level implementation strategy for Archie v1 defining three ordering principles (sources-before-projections, adopted-before-invented, highest-assumption-load-first), four-phase decomposition, leaf-task schema for small-model execution, and validation-gate mechanism for invented interactions.

nodes.name: bundle-size.json
nodes.summary: Bundle size measurement data tracking Archie's core library and rendering dependencies (OSD+Annotorious) against the 240KB gzip budget, documenting measured sizes for build-time monitoring.

nodes.name: archie.md
nodes.summary: Index of 7 constrained architectural decisions (Q-1 through Q-7) with stable citation IDs for the Archie project, covering exhibit-nested objects, 3-layer rendering with Svelte, annotation spine, and tiling strategy.

nodes.name: 00-INDEX.md
nodes.summary: Master index and coverage matrix for the entire Prior Art survey across 20 feature axes. Documents which repos were surveyed, identifies cross-cutting gaps, directs readers to the appropriate axis file for findings.

nodes.name: 01-deep-zoom-viewer.md
nodes.summary: Surveys OpenSeadragon deep-zoom viewer implementations across IIIF repos (cozy-iiif, liiive, field-studio, immarkus, osd-audio-video). Identifies gold extractables for viewport setup, tile-source switching, and coordinate reprojection, and flags the select-annotation-to-fitBounds gap as a key differentiator.

nodes.name: 02-annotation-tools.md
nodes.summary: Surveys annotation drawing tools (Annotorious, W3CImageFormat, shape editing) across field-studio, liiive, immarkus, browser-visual-search, and papadam. Documents the broken W3CImageFormat Ellipse/Path adapter and recommends SVG pre-parse mitigation as an unbuilt differentiator.

nodes.name: 03-annotation-data-model.md
nodes.summary: Surveys W3C Web Annotation Data Model implementations across annomea (gold standard for pure WADM utilities), cozy-iiif (AnnotationPage handling), immarkus (JSON-LD export), wadm-roundtrip, and papadam. Identifies gaps in multi-language Choice rendering and lossless non-rect SVG selector round-trips.

nodes.name: 04-multimedia-av.md
nodes.summary: Surveys audio/video annotation capabilities across osd-audio-video, videojs-annotation, quire, juncture, and papadam. Papadam provides the only real ASR transcript pipeline (Whisper-to-WebVTT) but stores transcripts as URL fields rather than WADM bodies, leaving the VTT-to-TextualBody adapter as unbuilt work.

nodes.name: 05-multi-object-collections.md
nodes.summary: Surveys IIIF Collection and multi-canvas patterns across cozy-iiif (gold for IIIF traversal), immarkus, biiif, tiny-iiif, tropy, and papadam. Covers cross-object exhibit navigation, manifest generation, and identifies the unified FSA-to-static-IIIF pipeline as the largest greenfield area.

nodes.name: 06-authoring-cms.md
nodes.summary: Surveys authoring and CMS approaches across the IIIF manifest editor, field-studio (closest sibling with Svelte editor and tiered persistence), immarkus, tropy, tiny-iiif, FossFLOW, and papadam (best server-backed multi-user CMS). Identifies gallery/exhibit-making UI and in-browser metadata management as key areas to build.

nodes.name: 07-published-read-ui.md
nodes.summary: Surveys published exhibit viewing UIs across juncture (prose-driven visual essay as scroll-sync gold), annomea (read-side anti-patterns to encode), liiive, Research-Narratives, quire, and papadam. No surveyed repo provides bidirectional scroll-sync with live annotation highlight on the prose side.

nodes.name: 08-linkable-navigable.md
nodes.summary: Surveys linkable and navigable annotation patterns across annomea (the 5-kind anvil:// URI scheme as gold), anvil (canonical patterns), quire (prose-to-region link contract), Research-Narratives, beehive, and papadam. Documents the fitBounds-plus-live-anchor-recompute gap as unbuilt across all surveyed repos.

nodes.name: 09-web-publishable-serverless.md
nodes.summary: Surveys serverless web publishing approaches across tiny-iiif, biiif, juncture, quire, canopy-export, and anvil. All surveyed IIIF generators are Node/Python-bound; no repo achieves in-browser serverless IIIF generation from FSA/OPFS, making this the largest greenfield area in the survey.

nodes.name: 10-state-mutation-patterns.md
nodes.summary: Surveys state-mutation and CRDT approaches across liiive (real-time collaborative IIIF with Yjs), annomea, excalidraw (command/delta inverse-entry undo), FossFLOW (snapshot undo in zustand), Graphite (full-document snapshot stacks), and papadam (best CRDT donor with production Y.js and server persistence bridge).

nodes.name: 11-20-NEW-AXES.md
nodes.summary: Defines and scopes the second-order feature axes (11-20) for the prior-art survey: search and discovery, accessibility, internationalization, import interop, provenance, performance, tile generation, embedding, AI assistance, and security. Names seven overflow candidate axes (21-27) for future exploration if scope warrants.

nodes.name: 11-search-discovery.md
nodes.summary: Surveys search and discovery approaches across browser-visual-search (region-to-similar-region ML as gold), field-studio (Content Search API 2.0 client with local full-text index), tropy (ALTO transcription ingest), clover/mirador (Content Search protocol), and papadam (server-side SQL full-text). No surveyed repo connects visual similarity search to existing annotations.

nodes.name: 12-accessibility.md
nodes.summary: Surveys accessibility and keyboard navigation across OpenSeadragon (built-in keyboard pan/zoom/rotate keymap), mirador (generic ARIA but no region-level a11y), annotorious (keyboard annotate with region-role signal), clover, universalviewer, field-studio, and papadam. No surveyed repo provides WCAG-compliant screen reader navigation for deep-zoom annotation regions.

nodes.name: 13-i18n-localization.md
nodes.summary: Surveys internationalization across IIIF manifesto (LanguageMap to display-string conversion as gold), mirador (i18next UI with the only real WADM Choice rendering), cozy-iiif (compact label helper), field-studio (RTL switch), quire, and papadam (Paraglide UI messages). No surveyed repo round-trips a multi-language annotation body through WADM.

nodes.name: 14-import-interop.md
nodes.summary: Surveys import and format interop across cozy-iiif (IIIF P2-to-P3 normalization), annotorious (W3C selector round-trip fidelity), tropy (OCR/ALTO ingest with JSON-LD round-trip), field-studio (spreadsheet and EXIF), mirador (legacy annotation lists), and papadam (tarball I/O). No surveyed repo achieves lossless non-rect geometry through WADM round-trip.

nodes.name: 15-provenance-citation.md
nodes.summary: Surveys provenance, citation, and versioning across annomea (WADM provenance fields emitted), anvil (content-hash naming as natural versioning), quire (citation-string generation as reusable logic), IIIF manifesto (requiredStatement and provider handling), tropy (EDTF dates), and papadam (immutable author/timestamp provenance). No repo provides document versioning for annotation data.

nodes.name: 16-performance-ux.md
nodes.summary: Surveys user-experienced performance patterns across mirador (virtualization and code-splitting), clover (lazy-load primitive), field-studio (build-time vendor splitting), universalviewer (content handler lazy import), cozy-iiif (resolution-aware resource model), and papadam (media-streaming and sync-write-batching). Static-host cold-start performance for serverless exhibits is unaddressed.

nodes.name: 17-tile-generation.md
nodes.summary: Surveys in-browser tile generation and image processing approaches across tiny-iiif (server-bound IIIF Image Server), biiif (Node sharp thumbnails), wax-tasks (Ruby build-time tiling), canopy (punts derivatives), field-studio (OffscreenCanvas with Worker as closest precedent but single-derivative only), and cozy-iiif (consumption-side image service classification). In-browser IIIF image pyramid generation from FSA does not exist in any surveyed repo.

nodes.name: 18-embedding-ecosystem.md
nodes.summary: Surveys embedding and ecosystem integration patterns across clover-iiif (modern React IIIF viewer that ships as a web component as gold), universalviewer (iframe-embed with embed-script generator), annomea and anvil (own embed surface), juncture (embed host framework), and mirador (config-object mount). No surveyed repo provides a serverless embed path that works without a hosting backend.

nodes.name: 19-ai-authoring.md
nodes.summary: Axis 19 prior-art survey examining AI-assisted authoring capabilities across 12 repos. Identifies papadam as the only repo with AI-authoring code, highlights IIIF/browser-visual-search as the in-browser AI gold (FastSAM+CLIP+onnxruntime-web), and documents gaps in AI integration that no surveyed repo solves.

nodes.name: 20-security-sanitization.md
nodes.summary: Axis 20 prior-art survey examining security and content sanitization patterns across 8 repos. Highlights field-studio's multi-layer sanitization at the boundary, annomea's hardened DOMPurify configuration, the IIIF/annotorious SvgSelector double-risk path, and decap-cms's backend-less OAuth as key references.

nodes.name: _COVERAGE.md
nodes.summary: Prior-art survey coverage sweep matrix mapping 25 repos across 20 survey axes. Documents which repos were cited per axis, identifies under-cited repos that warrant deeper survey, lists repos justified as out-of-scope, and flags surprising under-cited repos that contained real signal.

nodes.name: _DISPATCH-CONTEXT.md
nodes.summary: Shared dispatch context for prior-art survey agents, providing the repo inventory with axis-relevance mapping, output contract with citation requirements, file template, and wave 3 addendum covering newly cloned repos. Each axis surveyor consumed this file plus _FRAMING.md before working.

nodes.name: _FRAMING.md
nodes.summary: Framing brief for the prior-art survey encoding hard-won decisions from the user's own projects (anvil, annomea, field-studio). Documents the WADM round-trip verdict, locked annotation data model decisions, project-vs-image-level scope, storage/serverless architecture, glossary of exact terms, and per-axis look-for hints.
nodes.languageNotes: Load-bearing framing document that all axis surveyors must read before working.

nodes.name: _GAP-ANSWERS.md
nodes.summary: Close-loop document answering the 7 gap-targeting library clones identified in the survey. Provides resolution table, blocker status for each gap, integration-order recommendation, and caveman compression as a load-bearing check on the findings.

nodes.name: _GAPS.md
nodes.summary: Master gap ledger consolidating unsolved capabilities from all 20 survey axes. Includes a blocker shortlist of gaps that prevent honoring locked framing decisions, an orphan-hunt adjudication of candidate gaps not covered by any axis, and a caveman compression check.

nodes.name: _PAPADAM.md
nodes.summary: Single-repo cross-axis profile for papadam, evaluating it across all 20 survey axes. Identifies where papadam is the best donor among surveyed repos, where it fills previously-greenfield gaps, and provides a verdict on what to lift, study, or avoid for the Archie build.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Describes the @archie/studio authoring app as a Svelte SPA, its Phase 0 boundary-shell status, and Phase 2 plans to adopt anvil's editor components as running code.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: HTML entry point for the Studio SPA, loading Google Fonts and mounting the Svelte application at /src/main.ts.
nodes.languageNotes: Minimal HTML shell with a single div#app mount point and module script tag targeting the Vite dev server.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: Package configuration for the Studio workspace member declaring dependencies on @render/core, @render/svelte, and the Svelte/Vite toolchain for the authoring SPA.

nodes.name: 0001-objects-are-exhibit-nested.md
nodes.summary: Architecture Decision Record establishing that media objects and their annotations are owned by the Exhibit containing them, making each Exhibit self-contained and independently portable rather than drawing from a shared project-level pool.

nodes.name: 0002-rendering-and-framework.md
nodes.summary: Architecture Decision Record defining a three-layer headless rendering core (@render/core, @render/mount, @render/svelte adapters) and committing to Svelte for both the Studio and Viewer surfaces, with a strict ~500 LOC adapter budget serving as a logic-leak detector.

nodes.name: 0003-annotation-spine-append-only-version-dag.md
nodes.summary: Architecture Decision Record defining the annotation data model as an append-only log with version-parent DAG, enabling three-way merge without git and WADM-compatible heads/history serialization for scholarly citation integrity and async multi-author workflows.

nodes.name: 0004-no-wasm-vips-tiling.md
nodes.summary: Architecture Decision Record rejecting wasm-vips for deep-zoom tiling due to missing dzsave bindings and excessive bundle size (~13-20 MB vs 240 KB budget), opting instead for single responsive JPEG in v1 with an OffscreenCanvas DZI slicer planned for v1.1.

nodes.name: PHASE-0.md
nodes.summary: Implementation plan for Phase 0: project skeleton, workspace bootstrap, and the data-model spine (append-only log, version-DAG merge, heads-projection, WADM serialization), with the pure-WADM-consumer interop gate as the completion criterion.
nodes.languageNotes: Markdown decomposer plan with rigid task schema, acceptance gates, and ADR citations.

nodes.name: PHASE-1.md
nodes.summary: Implementation plan for Phase 1: extracting @render/core pure modules, @render/mount OSD+Annotorious wiring, and @render/svelte adapter from the anvil donor project, with the fitBounds-on-select behavioral gate as the acceptance criterion.
nodes.languageNotes: Markdown decomposer plan with characterization-testing strategy, donor file:line grounding, and LOC budget constraints.

nodes.name: PHASE-2.md
nodes.summary: Implementation plan for Phase 2: annotation persistence round-trip, IIIF projection, storage backends, publish-zip primitive, and a substantial backlog covering Studio UX bugs, object importing, real fixtures, and GitHub Pages publishing.
nodes.languageNotes: Markdown decomposer plan with source-vs-projection split, machine-verifiable vs browser/human task classification, and a multi-item backlog section.

nodes.name: pending.json
nodes.summary: Tracks documentation packages pending indexing by the deps-indexer, recording package names, registries, and resolution states for the Claude documentation caching system.

nodes.name: resolved.json
nodes.summary: Records successfully indexed documentation package entries with versions, freshness dates, and source URLs for the Claude dependency index cache.

nodes.name: .skill-invocation-log
nodes.summary: Records timestamps and skill names of every skill invocation in the Claude session for audit and signal detection.

nodes.name: deps-index.md
nodes.summary: Reference table mapping project libraries to their indexed documentation packages with freshness dates, source URLs, and known indexing failures for quick API lookup routing.

nodes.name: settings.json
nodes.summary: Claude plugin configuration enabling the understand-anything knowledge graph analysis plugin for the project.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Librarian skill definition for managing the project .claude/ directory -- organizing, auditing, splitting, cataloging, archiving, pruning, and persisting reference documentation across 10 duties with signal-driven triggers.
nodes.languageNotes: Defines 10 duties with pre-computed session-start signal caches that gate action rather than re-deriving state.

nodes.name: SUGGESTED_SKILLS.md
nodes.summary: Auto-generated skill recommendation list ranking candidate skills for the project based on detected signals, with codebase-diagnostics and check-handoff as the top suggestions.

nodes.name: store.db
nodes.summary: SQLite database backing the Foxhound hybrid search index for project knowledge retrieval with BM25 and vector embeddings.

nodes.name: store.db-shm
nodes.summary: Shared memory file for the Foxhound SQLite WAL-mode search index database, used for concurrent read access coordination.

nodes.name: store.db-wal
nodes.summary: Write-ahead log for the Foxhound SQLite search index database, recording recent transaction modifications before checkpoint.

nodes.name: .gitattributes
nodes.summary: Git attributes configuring merge=union strategy for mulch expertise and seeds issue jsonl files to avoid merge conflicts on concurrent edits.

nodes.name: system.md
nodes.summary: Design system specification for the Archie Viewer platform defining the curator's-study-at-night visual direction, depth strategy, palette tokens (canvas/paper/accent/semantic), typography scale, spacing, and component patterns with as-built component notes.
nodes.languageNotes: Two-surface token architecture (dark light-table canvas + warm paper panel) with vermillion accent as the curator's wax seal metaphor.

nodes.name: archie.q-counter
nodes.summary: Auto-incrementing counter for architected decision Q-N citations used across mulch decision records and seeds issues.

nodes.name: decisions.jsonl
nodes.summary: Mulch expertise records documenting architected decisions with shelf-life governance for the Archie project knowledge base.

nodes.name: infrastructure.jsonl
nodes.summary: Mulch expertise records tracking project infrastructure conventions and patterns for the Archie development environment.

nodes.name: meta.jsonl
nodes.summary: Mulch expertise meta-records tracking metadata about the knowledge base itself, including governance rules and classification defaults.

nodes.name: mulch.config.yaml
nodes.summary: Mulch knowledge management configuration defining three expertise domains (decisions, infrastructure, meta), entry count limits, and classification shelf-life defaults for tactical and observational records.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Quick-reference documentation for the mulch knowledge management system covering key CLI commands (init, add, search, setup) and directory structure.

nodes.name: config.yaml
nodes.summary: Seeds issue tracking configuration declaring the project name and configuration version for the Archie issue orchestration system.

nodes.name: issues.jsonl
nodes.summary: Seeds issue tracking database containing all project tasks with status, priority, dependency graph (blocks/blockedBy), close reasons, and labels for the Archie development workflow.

nodes.name: templates.jsonl
nodes.summary: Seeds issue template definitions for scaffolding new tasks with standardized workflows and metadata structures.

nodes.name: .understandignore
nodes.summary: Exclusion pattern file for the understand-anything codebase analyzer, defining glob patterns for files and directories to skip during knowledge graph extraction.

nodes.name: .gitkeep
nodes.summary: Empty placeholder file ensuring the apps/studio directory remains tracked in git for the Archie Studio SPA workspace.

nodes.name: App.svelte
nodes.summary: Main Studio editor component providing the full annotation workflow -- exhibit library management, OpenSeaDragon canvas annotation with draw tools, WADM-form note editing, persistence via OPFS, collaboration merge simulation, and GitHub Pages publishing.
nodes.languageNotes: Svelte 5 runes ($state, $derived, $effect) for reactive exhibit/annotation state management wrapping headless @render/core AnnotationSession.

nodes.name: LibraryHome.svelte
nodes.summary: Studio library home view displaying the exhibit gallery as a grid of cards with create-new-exhibit form, serving as the authoring index counterpart to the Viewer's Gallery.

nodes.name: openExhibit
nodes.summary: Opens an exhibit into the editor by loading per-exhibit annotation logs from OPFS, resolving imported-image assets to blob URLs, seeding default Voynich notes on first run, and surfacing any persisted merge conflicts.

nodes.name: addObjectFromFile
nodes.summary: Adds a local image file to the current exhibit by storing bytes in OPFS for persistence, creating a blob URL for display, probing natural dimensions, and appending the object metadata to the exhibit.

nodes.name: importChanges
nodes.summary: Simulates collaborative annotation merge by advancing a note locally then importing a concurrent colleague edit, producing merge conflicts for the review panel to demonstrate the version-DAG conflict resolution workflow.

nodes.name: main.ts
nodes.summary: Studio SPA entry point that mounts the App.svelte root component and imports global CSS including Annotorious/OpenSeadragon styles.

nodes.name: MergeReview.svelte
nodes.summary: Merge-review UI component that displays a summary panel and conflict resolution cards for collaborative annotation sessions, letting authors accept one version of conflicting edits.

nodes.name: Publish.svelte
nodes.summary: GitHub Pages publish dialog with a state machine (idle/publishing/done/error) that collects owner, repo, and PAT from the author and calls onpublish, never persisting the token.

nodes.name: store.ts
nodes.summary: OPFS-backed working store for the Studio that manages the project directory layout, library metadata persistence, and per-exhibit asset file CRUD using FsaFilesystem.
nodes.languageNotes: Browser-only OPFS FileSystemDirectoryHandle wrapped by FsaFilesystem -- works in the browser, not headless.

nodes.name: voynich.ts
nodes.summary: Generated fixture data for the Voynich manuscript exhibit (Beinecke MS 408) with five objects and their annotation regions, imported by scripts/import-voynich.mjs.

nodes.name: vite.config.ts
nodes.summary: Vite build configuration for the Studio Svelte SPA, configuring the Svelte plugin for the single-page application.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Project overview for the @archie/viewer package describing its Astro + Svelte islands architecture, Phase 0 boundary shell status, and Phase 2 adoption plans for annomea's narrative pane.

nodes.name: astro.config.mjs
nodes.summary: Astro build configuration for the Viewer static site with Svelte islands integration, targeting static output for GitHub Pages deployment.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: Viewer package manifest declaring dependencies on @render/core, @render/svelte, Annotorious, and Astro with dev and build scripts.

nodes.name: ExhibitView.svelte
nodes.summary: Exhibit layout orchestrator that loads published exhibit data, resolves layout (inferred grid for 3+ objects), and renders either an ObjectGrid overview or an object Reader.

nodes.name: ObjectGrid.svelte
nodes.summary: Exhibit object overview grid displaying thumbnails on a dark light table, with annotation counts and selection callback for object-led reading.

nodes.name: Reader.svelte
nodes.summary: Viewer reader island with a 3-state pane (note list, detail, and popup) over a read-only OpenSeadragon canvas, consuming published annotations in WADM format.

nodes.name: index.astro
nodes.summary: Viewer landing page rendering the library gallery as a card grid on a warm gallery wall, with exhibit title, description, and stats for each published exhibit.

nodes.name: sample.astro
nodes.summary: Exhibit reader page that renders the ExhibitView Svelte component as a client-only island for the Voynich manuscript exhibit.

nodes.name: published.ts
nodes.summary: Published exhibit loader that runs the sample library through publishLibrary into a MemoryFilesystem, then reads it back as a consumer would to prove the publish-render pipeline works identically with static files.

nodes.name: sample-data.ts
nodes.summary: Viewer sample exhibit data shaping the imported Voynich manuscript fixture into a typed Library with append-only annotation log for the publish pipeline.

nodes.name: voynich.ts
nodes.summary: Generated fixture data for the Voynich manuscript exhibit (Beinecke MS 408) with five objects and their annotation regions, imported by scripts/import-voynich.mjs.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: Root monorepo package.json defining workspace scripts (test, typecheck, build) and shared devDependencies for TypeScript and Vitest.

nodes.name: pnpm-workspace.yaml
nodes.summary: pnpm workspace configuration declaring packages/* and apps/* as workspace members.

nodes.name: tsconfig.base.json
nodes.summary: Base TypeScript compiler configuration with strict mode, ES2022 target, bundler module resolution, and verbatim module syntax shared across workspaces.

nodes.name: openExhibitAnnotationsDir
nodes.summary: Opens or creates the OPFS annotations directory for a given exhibit slug, using a legacy path for the sample exhibit and exhibits/{slug}/annotations for others.

nodes.name: loadLibraryMeta
nodes.summary: Reads and parses the library.json metadata file from the OPFS project directory.

nodes.name: saveLibraryMeta
nodes.summary: Serializes and writes the library metadata to library.json in the OPFS project directory.

nodes.name: saveAssetFile
nodes.summary: Writes an asset file (blob) to the exhibit's assets directory in OPFS.

nodes.name: readAssetUrl
nodes.summary: Reads an asset file from OPFS and creates an object URL for browser rendering.

nodes.name: readAssetBytes
nodes.summary: Reads raw bytes of an asset file from OPFS for programmatic processing.

nodes.name: loadPublishedExhibit
nodes.summary: Loads a published exhibit by running publishLibrary in-memory, reading the IIIF manifest and per-canvas annotations, and returning the structured exhibit with annotations grouped by object.

nodes.name: canvasIdFor
nodes.summary: Returns the IIIF canvas IRI for a given object ID using the publish pipeline's URI grammar.

nodes.name: getLog
nodes.summary: Returns the append-only annotation log for a given exhibit, used by the publish pipeline.

nodes.name: transcript.ts
nodes.summary: Parses WebVTT and SRT transcript formats into W3C annotation notes targeting AV media time ranges for supplementing annotation bodies.

nodes.name: parseCues
nodes.summary: Parses raw WebVTT/SRT text into structured cue objects with start/end timestamps and text content, handling both comma and period decimal separators.

nodes.name: cuesToNotes
nodes.summary: Converts parsed VTT/SRT cues into W3C supplementing annotation notes with media fragment selectors targeting specific time ranges.

nodes.name: importTranscript
nodes.summary: Imports transcript cues as supplementing annotation notes appended to an existing annotation log, producing time-ordered records.

nodes.name: orientation.test.ts
nodes.summary: Tests the EXIF orientation transform module, verifying all 8 EXIF orientation mappings, dimension normalization with axis-swaps, and noop detection.

nodes.name: orientation.ts
nodes.summary: Maps EXIF orientation tags (1-8) to geometric transforms for upright display-master normalization, including axis-swapping transpose and transverse cases.

nodes.name: conformance.test.ts
nodes.summary: Exercises all in-memory and zip-based filesystem backends against the shared conformance suite to verify seam compliance.

nodes.name: conformance.ts
nodes.summary: Shared filesystem conformance test suite that every backend (Memory, Zip, FSA) must satisfy identically, ensuring interchangeable behavior behind the Filesystem seam.

nodes.name: runConformance
nodes.summary: Registers a vitest describe block exercising read/write, nested directories, entries enumeration, overwrite, and error handling against any Filesystem backend factory.

nodes.name: fsa.ts
nodes.summary: File System Access API backend for the Filesystem seam, wrapping a user-picked directory handle for browser-based autosave-in-place operations.

nodes.name: FsaFile
nodes.summary: Wraps a FileSystemFileHandle providing readable/writable streams and File metadata access through the Filesystem seam interface.

nodes.name: FsaDir
nodes.summary: Wraps a FileSystemDirectoryHandle providing directory navigation, file creation, recursive removal, and async entry enumeration.

nodes.name: FsaFilesystem
nodes.summary: Filesystem seam adapter over a FileSystemDirectoryHandle, exposing a root directory for browser-based filesystem operations.

nodes.name: memory.ts
nodes.summary: In-memory Filesystem backend serving as the persistence round-trip oracle for unit tests and the playground ephemeral working store.

nodes.name: MemFile
nodes.summary: In-memory file implementation storing byte data as Uint8Array with readable/writable stream and File metadata support.

nodes.name: MemDir
nodes.summary: In-memory directory implementation using Maps for child files and directories with create-on-access semantics and async entry listing.

nodes.name: MemoryFilesystem
nodes.summary: Isolated in-memory filesystem root providing a MemDir for file and directory operations.

nodes.name: seam.test.ts
nodes.summary: Tests that the Filesystem seam interface contracts are coherent and implementable by exercising a throwaway in-memory backend.

nodes.name: seam.ts
nodes.summary: Declares the Filesystem seam interface -- the source abstraction that Memory, Zip, and FSA backends project against, defined first per source-before-projection rule.
nodes.languageNotes: Pure TypeScript interface declarations with no runtime code; the async iterable entries() method and File metadata mirroring demonstrate the DOM-friendly contract design.

nodes.name: zip.test.ts
nodes.summary: Tests the ZipFilesystem seam compliance, zip serialization round-trip, entry enumeration, and integration with the annotation spine persistence pipeline.

nodes.name: zip.ts
nodes.summary: ZipFilesystem implementation of the Filesystem seam over an in-memory flat file tree with fflate-based serialization to and from .archie.zip archives.

nodes.name: ZipFile
nodes.summary: Zip-backed file implementation reading/writing to an in-memory flat store with fflate string encoding and File metadata support.

nodes.name: ZipDir
nodes.summary: Zip-backed directory implementation with implicit directories from path prefixes, supporting getFile/getDirectory, recursive removal, and filtered entry enumeration.

nodes.name: ZipFilesystem
nodes.summary: Complete ZipFilesystem with root access, toZip serialization for saving .archie.zip files, and static fromZip factory for loading from bytes.

nodes.name: selector.test.ts
nodes.summary: Tests the pure selector parsing and geometry functions including fragment XYWH, polygon extraction, bounding box computation, degenerate value guarding, and shape labeling.

nodes.name: selector.ts
nodes.summary: Pure functions for parsing IIIF/W3C media fragment selectors and SVG polygon selectors into bounding boxes, with degenerate value detection and shape classification.

nodes.name: parseFragmentXYWH
nodes.summary: Parses a W3C media fragment xywh value into a bounding box, handling both pixel: and bare number formats with NaN validation.

nodes.name: parsePolygonPoints
nodes.summary: Extracts polygon points from an SVG selector value, returning null for degenerate values (NaN, empty, or insufficient points).

nodes.name: polygonBBox
nodes.summary: Computes the axis-aligned bounding box of a set of 2D points for fitBounds navigation contracts.

nodes.name: selectorBBox
nodes.summary: Unifies any W3C selector (rect FragmentSelector or SVG polygon) to a bounding box for use as fitBounds input.

nodes.name: isDegenerateSelectorValue
nodes.summary: Guards against degenerate Annotorious drawing outputs by detecting empty paths, empty polygons, and NaN values in selector strings.

nodes.name: shapeLabel
nodes.summary: Classifies a W3C selector value into a human-readable shape label (Rect, Polygon, Ellipse, Circle, Path, Line, or SVG) by inspecting the SVG content.

nodes.name: collection.ts
nodes.summary: Converts a Library model into a IIIF Presentation 3 Collection with manifest references for each exhibit and cover image thumbnails.

nodes.name: toCollection
nodes.summary: Transforms a Library into a IIIF Presentation 3 Collection, creating manifest item references with labels and cover thumbnails in library order.

nodes.name: resolve.test.ts
nodes.summary: Tests IIIF tile source resolution including plain image URLs, blob/data URLs, info.json endpoints, and IIIF Image API info object recognition.

nodes.name: resolve.ts
nodes.summary: Classifies image source strings into tile-source descriptors (direct image vs. IIIF info.json service) and recognizes pasted IIIF Image API info objects.

nodes.name: resolveTileSource
nodes.summary: Classifies an image source URL as direct image or external IIIF service, handling blob/data URLs to prevent spurious info.json appending.

nodes.name: isIiifImageInfo
nodes.summary: Type-narrows an unknown value by checking its @context property for a IIIF Image API context string (v2 or v3).

nodes.name: migrate.test.ts
nodes.summary: Tests for the schema migration runner, covering ordered migration application, idempotency on already-migrated documents, and schema version stamping for v1 baseline.

nodes.name: migrate.ts
nodes.summary: Schema migration runner that stamps schema version on persisted documents and applies ordered named migrations (tldraw pattern) to bring old docs forward. Provides the SCHEMA_VERSION constant, stamp helper, and migrate function with an empty v1 baseline registry.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses the tldraw-inspired named-migration pattern with an empty v1 baseline registry so the first real schema change just appends a Migration entry.

nodes.name: stamp
nodes.summary: Stamps the current SCHEMA_VERSION onto a document by spreading it with a schemaVersion property, ensuring all persisted documents are migratable.

nodes.name: migrate
nodes.summary: Applies every migration whose `to` exceeds the document's current version in ascending order, then stamps to at least SCHEMA_VERSION. Idempotent past the current version.

nodes.name: layout.test.ts
nodes.summary: Tests for layout resolution logic covering explicit author layout choice, inference from sections and object count, and descriptor field presence for narrative layouts.

nodes.name: layout.ts
nodes.summary: Layout resolution for exhibits: returns a LayoutDescriptor with type (single/grid/narrative) determined by the author's explicit choice or inferred from section and object counts. Pure function consumed by the UI renderer.

nodes.name: resolveLayout
nodes.summary: Resolves the layout type for an exhibit: explicit choice wins, otherwise infers narrative from sections, grid from multiple objects, or single as the fallback. Returns the type, objects, and optionally sections for narrative layouts.

nodes.name: model.ts
nodes.summary: Core Archie authoring domain model defining the Exhibit, AObject, Section, and Library types that the Studio authors and the IIIF projections derive from. Implements Exhibit-nested ownership (ADR-0001): an Exhibit owns its Objects with no shared object pool.
nodes.languageNotes: Pure TypeScript type and interface definitions with no runtime code; drives the entire authoring model for exhibit/object/section structures.

nodes.name: ghpages.ts
nodes.summary: GitHub Pages publish adapter that pushes a published file tree to a gh-pages branch via the GitHub git-trees API. Binary-aware: JSON pages use inline text content, image assets are base64-encoded and uploaded as git blobs referenced by SHA.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses the GitHub git-trees API directly from the browser for a serverless publish flow; binary assets are uploaded as base64 blobs while text content stays inline in the tree payload.

nodes.name: collectFiles
nodes.summary: Recursively flattens a published FsDirectory into a path-to-FileContent map, classifying files as text or base64-encoded binary based on extension.

nodes.name: buildGitTree
nodes.summary: Builds the GitHub git-trees API payload from a file map, producing sorted entries with inline content for text or base64 for binary files.

nodes.name: publishToGitHub
nodes.summary: Pushes a published file tree to a GitHub Pages branch via the git-trees API: uploads binary blobs, creates a tree from the base commit, creates a commit, and updates the branch ref.

nodes.summary: File no longer exists on disk. Previously exported manifest projection helpers (toManifest, storyToCanvas) for converting Archie exhibit models to IIIF Presentation API manifests.

nodes.name: wadm.ts
nodes.summary: File no longer exists on disk. Previously provided WADM (Web Annotation Data Model) integration or annotation format bridging for the Archie annotation system.

nodes.name: library.ts
nodes.summary: File no longer exists on disk. Previously exported the publishLibrary function for publishing entire exhibit libraries to their target format.

nodes.name: manifest.ts

nodes.summary: File no longer exists on disk. Previously contained tests for zip-format publish functionality.

nodes.summary: File no longer exists on disk. Previously defined TypeScript types and interfaces for the publish subsystem.

nodes.name: collection.test.ts
nodes.summary: File no longer exists on disk. Previously contained tests for IIIF collection resolution functionality.

nodes.name: collection.ts
nodes.summary: File no longer exists on disk. Previously exported collection resolution helpers (buildAnnotationsIndex, buildObjectMap, collectionOf, collectionIdForExhibit, resolveCollection) for IIIF collection building.

nodes.name: iiif.test.ts
nodes.summary: File no longer exists on disk. Previously contained tests for IIIF tile source resolution.

nodes.name: iiif.ts
nodes.summary: File no longer exists on disk. Previously exported IIIF resolution functions (resolveTileSource, resolveTiles, isServiceType, isImageServiceInfo, isImageURI) for image tile source handling.

nodes.name: image-info.test.ts

nodes.name: image-info.ts
nodes.summary: File no longer exists on disk. Previously exported image info helpers (getMaxLevelInfo, getInfoForLevel, compatibleSizes) for IIIF image service resolution.

nodes.name: resolve.ts
nodes.summary: File no longer exists on disk. Previously exported the resolveCanvases function for IIIF canvas resolution.

nodes.name: tiling-scenarios.test.ts
nodes.summary: File no longer exists on disk. Previously contained tests for IIIF tiling scenario edge cases.

nodes.name: point.ts
nodes.summary: File no longer exists on disk. Previously provided point-based annotation selector logic.

nodes.name: polygon.ts
nodes.summary: File no longer exists on disk. Previously provided polygon-based annotation selector logic.

nodes.name: to-fragment.ts
nodes.summary: File no longer exists on disk. Previously converted annotation selectors to fragment strings.

nodes.name: to-svg.ts
nodes.summary: File no longer exists on disk. Previously converted annotation selectors to SVG path representations.

nodes.name: persist.ts
nodes.summary: Provides annotation log persistence through the Filesystem seam — writes the consumer heads page and history sidecar to disk, and reloads the full DAG from history pages.
nodes.languageNotes: Idempotent projection of the annotation log: write returns a deterministic result regardless of prior state.

nodes.name: writeAnnotations
nodes.summary: Serializes the annotation log to disk: heads page, history index, and per-logicalId history pages.

nodes.name: readAnnotations
nodes.summary: Reloads the full append-only annotation log from disk by reconstructing the DAG from history pages.

nodes.name: resolve.test.ts
nodes.summary: Tests merge resolution (Q-7) that collapses plural concurrent heads into a single multi-parent merge node, verifying DAG walk across both branches.

nodes.name: serialize.test.ts
nodes.summary: Tests WADM serialization: record-to-annotation round-trips for rect and polygon shapes, heads page citation id stability, and full-history version chain output.

nodes.name: serialize.ts
nodes.summary: History-sidecar WADM serialization: produces consumer heads pages (current versions only) and per-logicalId history pages with collison-disambiguated citation ids.
nodes.languageNotes: Q-6 collision rule: shared (logicalId, version) disambiguated by lexicographic rev order with ~rev suffix for all but the lowest.

nodes.name: dedupe
nodes.summary: Deduplicates an annotation log by rev, preserving first-seen order.

nodes.name: citationIds
nodes.summary: Maps every record's rev to its collision-disambiguated citation id (Q-6).

nodes.name: recordToAnnotation
nodes.summary: Builds a base WADM Annotation from a record with an explicit id.

nodes.name: withDagMeta
nodes.summary: Embeds archie DAG metadata as extension fields on history annotations.

nodes.name: targetSource
nodes.summary: Extracts the source IRI (canvas id) from an annotation record's target.

nodes.name: headsPageFromRecords
nodes.summary: Builds a heads AnnotationPage from a pre-selected set of head records with prov links and history links.

nodes.name: toHeadsPage
nodes.summary: Produces the canvas heads page: current head versions per logicalId with citation and provenance links.

nodes.name: citationIdMap
nodes.summary: Builds a rev-to-citation-id map for the full log, shared by per-canvas publish.

nodes.name: toHistory
nodes.summary: Produces the history sidecar: per-logicalId AnnotationPages with full version chains plus an index map.

nodes.name: brand.ts
nodes.summary: Defines branded ULID-based id types for the annotation spine: LogicalId, RevId, VersionId, ClientId, and ExhibitId with mint/parse/validate functions.
nodes.languageNotes: Brand pattern uses a phantom unique symbol for nominally-distinct string subtypes that erase at build time.

nodes.name: encodeTime
nodes.summary: Encodes a timestamp as a Crockford base32 ULID time component with validation.

nodes.name: mintLogicalId
nodes.summary: Mints a fresh ULID logical id with injectable now/rng for deterministic tests.

nodes.name: mintRevId
nodes.summary: Mints a fresh per-record DAG node id (ULID), distinct from the logical id for collision-free parent pointers.

nodes.name: asRevId
nodes.summary: Validates and brands an existing ULID string as a RevId.

nodes.name: asLogicalId
nodes.summary: Validates and brands an existing ULID string as a LogicalId.

nodes.name: versionId
nodes.summary: Builds a resolvable version id string from a logicalId and version number.

nodes.name: parseVersionId
nodes.summary: Parses a version id back into its logical id and version number with validation.

nodes.name: asClientId
nodes.summary: Brands a non-empty string as a ClientId for identifying authors/agents.

nodes.name: asExhibitId
nodes.summary: Brands a non-empty string as an ExhibitId for IIIF Manifest scope identity.

nodes.name: manifest.json
nodes.summary: Test corpus specification for EXIF orientation normalization — defines 8 orientation transforms with swap/noop flags and expected image dimensions.
nodes.languageNotes: Consumer status is NOT-WIRED; the manifest serves as the spec that the EXIF normalize code will assert against.

nodes.name: resolve.ts
nodes.summary: Annotation spine conflict resolution — planned module for collapsing concurrent heads into a unified version DAG.

nodes.name: write.test.ts
nodes.summary: Tests for the spine write/persistence operations — planned test file.

nodes.name: write.ts
nodes.summary: Annotation spine write operations — planned module for saving and deleting annotations.

nodes.name: broadcast.ts
nodes.summary: Annotation state broadcast — planned module for propagating state changes across clients.

nodes.name: heads.ts
nodes.summary: Annotation state heads tracking — planned module for managing current head versions.

nodes.name: merge.ts
nodes.summary: Annotation state merge resolution — planned module for classifying and resolving concurrent edits.

nodes.name: write.ts
nodes.summary: Annotation state write operations — planned module for persisting state-layer annotations.

nodes.name: indices.ts
nodes.summary: WADM index helpers — planned module for indexing annotation records.

nodes.name: write.test.ts
nodes.summary: Tests for WADM write operations — planned test file for annotation log writing.

nodes.name: write.ts
nodes.summary: WADM annotation log write operations — planned module for writing annotation logs.

nodes.summary: WADM round-trip test fixture — provides annotation test data for serialization validation.

nodes.name: Canvas.svelte
nodes.summary: Thin Svelte 5 shell component wrapping @render/mount, wiring the mount surface to reactive $state, drawing props, and lifecycle callbacks. All binding/edit logic lives in the headless createCanvasController for testability.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Svelte 5 runes ($state, $effect, $bindable, $props) with a headless TypeScript controller for testability.

nodes.name: controller.test.ts
nodes.summary: Tests for the CanvasController selection binding logic, verifying programmatic select drives surface methods, inbound user selection updates state without feedback loops, subscriber notification works for both directions, and destroy tears down correctly.

nodes.name: controller.ts
nodes.summary: Headless CanvasController for the Svelte adapter providing programmatic selection, inbound user-selection notification, subscriber management, and surface lifecycle teardown. Plain TypeScript so it is fully testable with a mock MountSurface.

nodes.name: link.ts
nodes.summary: Citation resolution for render-svelte (walking skeleton -- file not yet implemented, declared in ADR-0002).

nodes.name: scrub.test.ts

nodes.languageNotes: happy-dom environment required because OpenSeadragon accesses document at import time, not just during viewer construction.

layers.name: Core Library
layers.description: Core domain model, annotation spine with version DAG, WADM serialization, filesystem abstractions (Memory/Zip/FSA), IIIF collection and tile resolution, geometry selectors, EXIF orientation, transcript parsing, and schema migration for the Archie platform

layers.name: Render Adapters
layers.description: Framework-free mount surface wiring OpenSeadragon and Annotorious, plus the Svelte 5 adapter providing the Canvas component and headless controller for reactive UI integration

layers.name: Applications
layers.description: Studio SPA for authoring exhibits with annotation tools, OPFS persistence, and GitHub Pages publishing, plus the Viewer SSG for consuming published exhibits as static sites

layers.name: Documentation
layers.description: Architecture decision records, implementation phase plans, design documents, session continuity notes, and project onboarding guides for the Archie platform

layers.name: Research
layers.description: Prior art survey covering 20 axes across 25 IIIF annotation and exhibit-publishing repos, identifying gaps and gold extractables for the Archie platform

layers.name: Project Configuration
layers.description: Monorepo build configuration, TypeScript compiler settings, pnpm workspace definition, and git attributes shared across all Archie packages and applications

layers.name: Developer Tooling
layers.description: Claude Code configuration and skills, mulch knowledge base, seeds issue tracker, foxhound search index, interface design system, and understand-anything analysis artifacts for the Archie project

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: CONTEXT.md serves as the comprehensive design document for Archie, defining its core vocabulary (Library, Exhibit, Object, Note, Layer) and locked technical requirements: OpenSeadragon + Annotorious for deep-zoom image annotation, the W3C Web Annotation Data Model (WADM) for annotation representation, and IIIF Presentation 3 for on-disk storage. It establishes the architectural through-line of 'source-before-projection' -- the principle that domain models are authored first and then projected into IIIF formats, rather than being derived from them. This document is the starting point for understanding the static-publishable, multi-media exhibit annotation platform.

tour.title: Architecture Foundation
tour.description: Architecture Decision Record ADR-0002 establishes the three-layer rendering architecture that shapes every component in the system: @render/core (pure TypeScript logic with no DOM dependencies), @render/mount (framework-free mount functions wiring OpenSeadragon + Annotorious + Wavesurfer), and @render/svelte (a thin Svelte 5 adapter). A strict ~500 LOC budget on the Svelte adapter acts as a logic-leak detector, ensuring that all binding and editing logic remains in the headless layers where it is fully testable. This architecture enables both the Studio (authoring SPA) and Viewer (published static site) to share the same rendering pipeline.

tour.title: Core Domain Model
tour.description: The domain model in model.ts defines the core Archie authoring types: Exhibit, AObject (media object), Section, and Library. Per ADR-0001, Exhibits own their Objects with no shared object pool -- each Exhibit is a self-contained, independently portable unit. The layout.ts module provides pure-function layout resolution that determines whether an exhibit displays as single, grid, or narrative layout based on the author's explicit choice or inference from section and object counts. These types form the vocabulary that the entire Studio authoring workflow and all IIIF projections derive from.

tour.title: Annotation Spine Identity
tour.description: The brand.ts module defines the fundamental identity primitives for the annotation spine using branded ULID types: LogicalId (stable identity of an annotation across revisions), RevId (a single revision), VersionId (a concrete version in the DAG), ClientId (author identity), and ExhibitId. These branded types are imported by nearly every module in the core library (fan-out of 17, the highest in the codebase) because they provide type-safe identity throughout the annotation versioning system. The mint, parse, and validate functions ensure that every ID is both human-readable and machine-verifiable.
tour.languageLesson: TypeScript branded types use intersection types (e.g., `type LogicalId = string & { readonly __brand: 'LogicalId' }`) to create nominal typing at zero runtime cost. The brand exists only at compile time -- at runtime, values are just strings.

tour.title: Annotation Spine and Persistence
tour.description: The annotation spine (ADR-0003) implements an append-only log with a version-parent DAG, enabling three-way merge without git. The serialize.ts module produces WADM-compatible consumer heads pages (current versions only) and per-logicalId history pages with collision-disambiguated citation IDs. The persist.ts module writes these pages through the Filesystem seam -- writing the heads page and history sidecar to disk, and reloading the full DAG from history pages. This design supports scholarly citation integrity and async multi-author workflows, where concurrent edits produce plural heads that can later be merged into a single multi-parent merge node.

tour.title: Geometry and Selectors
tour.description: The selector.ts module provides pure functions for parsing IIIF/W3C media fragment selectors (xywh=pixel) and SVG polygon selectors into unified bounding boxes. It handles degenerate value detection (empty paths, NaN values from Annotorious drawing outputs) and classifies selectors into shape labels (Rect, Polygon, Ellipse, Circle, Path, Line, SVG). This module is the bridge between Annotorious's drawing output and the annotation spine's storage format, ensuring that any shape an author draws can be faithfully captured, persisted, and later displayed in the Viewer.

tour.title: Filesystem Abstraction Layer
tour.description: The Filesystem seam (seam.ts) is the most depended-upon interface in the codebase (fan-in of 6), following the 'source-before-projection' principle: the interface is defined first, then all backends project against it. Three implementations exist: MemoryFilesystem for unit tests and ephemeral working stores, ZipFilesystem for persistent .archie.zip archives with fflate-based serialization, and FsaFilesystem wrapping the browser's File System Access API for autosave-in-place operations. A shared conformance test suite (conformance.ts) exercises every backend identically, ensuring interchangeable behavior behind the seam.

tour.title: IIIF Projection
tour.description: The IIIF modules project the authored domain model into standards-compliant formats for consumption by IIIF-compatible viewers. The collection.ts module converts a Library into a IIIF Presentation 3 Collection with manifest references and cover image thumbnails for each exhibit. The resolve.ts module classifies image source strings into tile-source descriptors -- detecting direct image URLs versus IIIF Image API info.json services, and recognizing pasted IIIF Image API info objects. These projections allow Archie exhibits to be consumed by any IIIF-compatible viewer, not just Archie's own Viewer.

tour.title: Render Adapter Layer
tour.description: The render adapter layer bridges the headless core with UI frameworks. The controller.ts module provides the headless CanvasController with programmatic selection, inbound user-selection notification via subscribers, and surface lifecycle teardown -- all in plain TypeScript for full testability. Canvas.svelte is the thin Svelte 5 shell component that wraps @render/mount's imperative MountSurface, wiring reactive $state, drawing props, and lifecycle callbacks. This design ensures that the Svelte adapter is kept under the 500 LOC budget, preventing framework-specific logic from leaking into the shared rendering core.
tour.languageLesson: Svelte 5 uses runes ($state, $derived, $effect) for explicit reactivity instead of the compiler-magic assignments in Svelte 4. The Canvas.svelte component wraps headless logic so the Svelte layer stays thin -- a pattern that prevents framework coupling in reusable rendering code.

tour.title: Authoring Studio Application
tour.description: The Studio is a Svelte 5 SPA authored in the browser. Its entry point (main.ts) mounts the App.svelte root component, which orchestrates the full annotation workflow: exhibit library management, OpenSeadragon canvas annotation with draw tools, WADM-form note editing, and OPFS-backed persistence. The store.ts module provides the OPFS-backed working store using FsaFilesystem, managing the project directory layout, library metadata persistence, and per-exhibit asset file CRUD. The Studio is where curators author exhibits -- importing images, drawing annotation regions, and writing scholarly notes -- before publishing the result as a static site.

tour.title: Published Viewer Application
tour.description: The Viewer is an Astro-built static site (SSG) that consumes published exhibits. Its landing page (index.astro) renders the library gallery as a card grid on a warm gallery wall. The Reader.svelte component is the core consumption surface: a 3-state pane (note list, detail, and popup) over a read-only OpenSeadragon canvas, consuming published annotations in WADM format. ExhibitView.svelte acts as the layout orchestrator, loading published exhibit data, resolving the layout type (single/grid/narrative), and rendering either an ObjectGrid overview or an object-level Reader. The Astro + Svelte islands architecture ensures that interactive components load only where needed, keeping the static site fast.

tour.title: Publishing Pipeline
tour.description: The publishing pipeline bridges the gap between authoring and distribution entirely in the browser. The ghpages.ts module implements a serverless GitHub Pages publish adapter that pushes a published file tree to a gh-pages branch via the GitHub git-trees API -- JSON pages use inline text content while image assets are base64-encoded and uploaded as git blobs referenced by SHA. The Publish.svelte dialog in the Studio wraps this with a state machine (idle/publishing/done/error) that collects owner, repo, and personal access token from the author, never persisting the token. This pipeline is the key differentiator: in-browser, serverless IIIF generation and deployment without any server infrastructure.
tour.languageLesson: The GitHub git-trees API allows creating commits entirely from the browser without a local git client. The serverless publish flow builds git tree objects from an in-memory file system, creates blobs for binary assets, and pushes a branch reference -- all via authenticated REST calls, with no server-side processing.
